behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names all required imports, function names, test class/method names, and patch targets that the tests assert on.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction essentially enumerates the fix: which imports to add/remove, to call resolve_org_for_repo, use uuid4().hex, call SaasConversationStore.get_resolver_instance, call start_conversation, and not call create_new_conversation — leaving little reasoning about WHAT to change.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check for the required functions/classes/kwarg (git_provider) which any correct implementation would share.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: F2P tests only parse AST and check for imports/calls/strings (e.g., `uuid4().hex` in source); no tests actually execute create_or_update_conversation behavior.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests require specific imports and call patterns that a stub returning None couldn't satisfy without implementing the routing.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Instruction requires routing behavior (claimed org vs personal workspace), but the hidden oracle only checks AST/text patterns and test-file contents, not runtime routing behavior.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section comments ('Fail-to-Pass Tests', 'Pass-to-Pass Tests') and descriptive names.

no_typos.reason: Paths and names are consistent across instruction, tests, and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names the exact file (`enterprise/integrations/jira/jira_view.py`), exact function, and exact replacement APIs, effectively pre-localizing the fix.
